constraints: Human lead retains final product code asset scope and integration authority
Use AI agents for bounded planning implementation test documentation tooling and content tasks
Allow selective specialist outsourcing for art audio music animation or similar needs
Persist operational knowledge and decisions in the repository
Support aggressive concurrency only for independently owned tasks
Expose dependencies file ownership interfaces and acceptance criteria before work begins
Keep review and integration load manageable for the solo lead
Do not use parallelism to justify overlapping speculative implementations
Give every agent a narrow branch scope owned modules dependencies interfaces and acceptance tests
Review and merge tasks individually through controlled integration
Avoid concurrent edits to the same Unity serialized assets unless explicitly sequenced
Require reproducible evidence for acceptance
Organize ownership around player weapons enemies levels rewards menus and shared foundations
Prefer tasks that remain inside one bounded domain
Govern cross-domain damage item event save and content-reference contracts explicitly
Validate domains through playable vertical integrations
Build the Windows-first internal MVP in Unity with C#
Preserve a credible future Android export path without implementing Android now
Prefer project-owned C# contracts over unnecessary proprietary package coupling
Do not adopt DOTS or a custom engine without measured need
Keep important gameplay rules and mutable domain state in plain testable C# where practical
Use Unity components for input physics rendering animation audio and scene lifecycle
Avoid implicit scene searches lifecycle coupling and conflicting authoritative state
Leave a clean later co-op adaptation path without implementing multiplayer now
Use typed ScriptableObjects for immutable weapon enemy loot encounter and difficulty definitions
Keep mutable runtime state in plain C# objects
Provide stable IDs reference conventions and validation
Keep serialized definition assets small and domain-owned
Defer external modding data pipelines until required
Use URP 2D lights sprite normal maps shadows emissive effects and reactive combat lighting
Keep gameplay readability independent of dynamic light availability
Integrate normal-map production with the offline-3D sprite pipeline
Define performance budgets and fallback quality for future Android
Keep runtime presentation fully two-dimensional
Separate persistent systems UI level geometry lighting encounters and focused tests
Assign explicit temporary ownership of every edited serialized asset
Use explicit cross-scene initialization and references
Support focused verification scenes
Preserve handcrafted visual authoring rather than code-generating the level
Use plain-C# unit tests for domain rules where applicable
Use targeted Unity tests for engine integration
Require automated project or build validation
Require a playable proof or precise reproducible procedure for user-visible behavior
Reserve experiential judgement for human review
Match test depth to task risk and add practical regression coverage

open_questions_added: Windows MVP keyboard mouse gamepad and rebinding baseline
Unity version package pinning and upgrade policy
Source asset and generated sprite storage policy
Offline 3D modeling rigging rendering and sprite-export toolchain
Exact first-level zone and checkpoint structure
Minimal strongbox shop currency inventory and duplicate handling
Audio music voice and localization production boundary
Performance budgets for Windows and future Android
Save format migration and compatibility policy
CI provider and automated Unity licensing setup

next_action.instruction: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether the Windows-first internal MVP supports keyboard and mouse only, keyboard and mouse plus gamepad with fully rebindable actions, or keyboard mouse gamepad and an early touch-control abstraction for future Android. Preserve Unity with C#, the hybrid domain-core architecture, four simultaneously equipped weapons, directional thruster burst, shallow angled top-down flat-plane play, permanent offline play, Windows-first delivery, and future Android support. Include aiming precision, twin-stick suitability, accessibility, rebinding, UI prompt switching, test burden, Input System architecture, future touch adaptation, scope cost, and risk of delaying mobile-specific work. Put the recommendation after all A/B/C options at the end of the card. Queue D-161 through D-170 and persist at D-170 unless the user changes the batching rule or requests a context handoff.
